session: Production Launch — Blue/Green Deploy to ectropy.ai

issues_resolved.title: GitHub Variables not configured
issues_resolved.symptom: Validate Prerequisites failed: DATABASE_CLUSTER_ID not set
issues_resolved.root_cause: All 16 GitHub repository variables (vars.*) were empty — never configured
issues_resolved.fix: Set 16 variables via `gh variable set` using values queried from `doctl`: PROD_BLUE_IP, PROD_GREEN_IP, PROD_BLUE_DROPLET_ID, PROD_GREEN_DROPLET_ID, PROD_LB_ID, PROD_FIREWALL_ID, DATABASE_CLUSTER_ID, DATABASE_HOST, DATABASE_PORT, DATABASE_NAME, DATABASE_USER, API_URL, FRONTEND_URL

issues_resolved.title: SSH key environment scoping — repo-level vs production environment
issues_resolved.symptom: Setup SSH key step failed with 'Error loading key (stdin): error in libcrypto'
issues_resolved.root_cause: deploy-blue job declares `environment: production`, so GitHub environment-level secrets override repo-level. All `gh secret set PROD_SSH_KEY` calls without `--env` went to repo-level, while the stale environment-level key (from March 12) was always used instead.
issues_resolved.fix: Generated new RSA 4096 PEM key, added public key to authorized_keys on both servers, set private key with `gh secret set PROD_SSH_KEY --env production`
issues_resolved.five_why: WHY-1: libcrypto error → WHY-2: stale ED25519 key in environment → WHY-3: environment secrets override repo-level → WHY-4: `gh secret set` defaults to repo-level → WHY-5: no --env flag used

issues_resolved.title: Missing docker-compose.deploy.yml
issues_resolved.symptom: Deploy to blue server failed: docker-compose.deploy.yml not found
issues_resolved.root_cause: deploy-to-server.sh (line 233) expects docker-compose.deploy.yml but the file was never created. docker-compose.production.yml already uses image: directives (no build:), so it IS the deploy config.
issues_resolved.fix: Created docker-compose.deploy.yml as copy of docker-compose.production.yml. Added .gitignore exception (!docker-compose.deploy.yml). Committed to main.

issues_resolved.title: Stale DB passwords in production environment secrets
issues_resolved.symptom: P1000: Authentication failed against database server, the provided database credentials for 'doadmin' are not valid
issues_resolved.root_cause: DB_ADMIN_PASSWORD and DB_PASSWORD in GitHub production environment were set on March 12 and had since been rotated by DigitalOcean managed database.
issues_resolved.fix: Queried current passwords via `doctl databases connection` and updated both secrets with `--env production`

issues_resolved.title: Prisma migration P3009 — failed migration blocks all retries
issues_resolved.symptom: P3009: migrate found failed migrations in the target database, new migrations will not be applied. Migration 20260313000000_sprint5_ros_mro_schema_drift recorded as failed.
issues_resolved.root_cause: Sprint5 migration ran once, failed mid-execution (SQL error), and Prisma recorded a 'failed' row in _prisma_migrations. All subsequent retries (including container restarts) see P3009 and refuse to proceed. The original SQL error was lost because CI only captures last 50 lines of container logs.
fix_code: Made migration SQL idempotent: DO/EXCEPTION for CREATE TYPE, IF NOT EXISTS for ADD VALUE/ADD COLUMN/CREATE TABLE/CREATE INDEX, exception guards for FK constraints
Added P3009 auto-resolve to docker-entrypoint.sh: detects P3009, deletes failed row from _prisma_migrations via prisma db execute (with psql fallback), then retries

issues_resolved.title: SESSION_SECRET too short (30 chars, min 32)
issues_resolved.symptom: FATAL: Bootstrap Failed — SECURITY: SESSION_SECRET must be at least 32 characters (current: 30)
issues_resolved.root_cause: SESSION_SECRET in production environment was only 30 characters. The api-gateway config/index.ts validates minimum 32 characters in production.
issues_resolved.fix: Generated new 64-char hex SESSION_SECRET via `openssl rand -hex 32`, set with `--env production`

issues_resolved.title: JWT_SECRET too short (62 chars, min 64)
issues_resolved.symptom: FATAL: Bootstrap Failed — SECURITY: JWT_SECRET should be at least 64 characters for production use (current: 62)
issues_resolved.root_cause: JWT_SECRET in production environment was 62 characters. The api-gateway config/index.ts validates minimum 64 characters for production.
issues_resolved.fix: Generated new 96-char hex secrets via `openssl rand -hex 48` for both JWT_SECRET and JWT_REFRESH_SECRET, set with `--env production`

issues_resolved.title: @luh-tech/crm 401 in CI post-deployment validation
issues_resolved.root_cause: Post-Deployment Validation job runs `pnpm install` which needs LUHTECH_PKG_READ token for @luh-tech/crm GitHub Package. Token not set as a secret accessible to the deploy workflow.
issues_resolved.fix: TODO: Set LUHTECH_PKG_READ as a repository secret or production environment secret. Non-blocking — actual deployment succeeded.

secondary_fixes.title: MCP server Redis ECONNREFUSED 127.0.0.1
secondary_fixes.description: MCP server code reads REDIS_HOST env var (defaulting to 127.0.0.1) but docker-compose only set REDIS_URL (which MCP ignores). Added REDIS_HOST=redis, REDIS_PORT=6379, REDIS_PASSWORD to MCP server environment in both docker-compose.deploy.yml and docker-compose.production.yml.

commits_to_main.message: fix(deploy): make sprint5 migration idempotent + P3009 auto-resolve
fix(deploy): add REDIS_HOST/PORT to MCP server compose environment

pending_items: Backport main commits to develop branch
Set LUHTECH_PKG_READ secret for deploy workflow (P1)
Speckle server DB connection timeout (speckle database may not exist on managed cluster)
Production data seeding (Canadian pilot demo data)
Migrate production deploy to Zero-SSH Watchtower pattern (matching staging)
